key_drivers: Real yields flat at 1.92% (DFII10) — no directional boost for gold or tech, but not a headwind
Credit spreads narrowing (HY OAS 2.85%) and yield curve steepening (2s10s 0.51%) strongly bullish for small caps and value equities
SOX surging (+26.62% 1 month) and MOVE falling (-35.80% 1 month) support Nasdaq and risk assets broadly
US-Iran ceasefire extended indefinitely by Trump (Apr 22), reducing immediate supply shock risk for crude
BoJ holding at 0.75% with dropping hawkish signals keeps JPY rate differential wide against USD — bearish 6J
Gold ETF inflows positive for 3 consecutive weeks in April after record March outflows

fallbacks_used: TradingView FRED-DFII10 for real yields confirmation
data_quality.notes: FOMC meeting April 30 — 6 days away. Pre-FOMC uncertainty reduces confidence by 1 point across equity instruments. All data sourced from live market feeds as of 2026-04-23 07:36 EDT.
